description: Fix four bugs in the MCP server's browser tools: (1) analyze-site's 25-second observation sleep is not cancellable, leaving a tab and active network capture open for up to 25 extra seconds after the MCP dispatch times out; also `executeInTab` discards valid JSON values that happen to start with `{` or `[`; (2) `activeNetworkCaptures` is tracked on the wrong connection (getAnyConnection instead of the tab's owning connection), causing leaks when connections differ; (3) extension-get-logs produces unstable sort order when log entries lack a `timestamp` field; (4) plugin-list-tabs `hostnameFromPattern` only handles `*://<host>/*` — patterns like `https://*.example.com/*` or `http://localhost:3000/*` are silently skipped, breaking multi-instance instance annotation.

userStories.title: Make analyze-site observation sleep cancellable and fix executeInTab JSON discard
userStories.description: In analyze-site/index.ts, step 4 runs `await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, waitSeconds * 1000))`. When the outer MCP dispatch times out (30s), the `finally` block runs only after the sleep resolves, holding the network capture and analysis tab open for up to 25 additional seconds. Also fix `executeInTab`: its heuristic that discards strings starting with `{` or `[` (intended to filter Chrome's partial-JSON truncation artifact) also discards intentionally short JSON objects, silently returning null.
acceptanceCriteria: The observation sleep in analyze-site is implemented with a cancellable timer (e.g., using AbortSignal or a manual resolve/cancel pair); when the tool's AbortSignal fires or the surrounding code cancels, the sleep resolves immediately

`executeInTab` does NOT discard string results that begin with `{` or `[` when the string is valid JSON; the Chrome truncation artifact (a raw non-closed `{` or `[`) is filtered differently (e.g., by attempting JSON.parse and checking for SyntaxError)
The tab opened by analyze-site and its network capture are cleaned up in the `finally` block promptly after MCP dispatch timeout

userStories.title: Fix activeNetworkCaptures tracking to use the tab's owning connection
userStories.description: In enable-network-capture.ts and analyze-site/index.ts, `activeNetworkCaptures.add(tabId)` and `.delete(tabId)` are called on `getAnyConnection(state)` instead of the connection that owns the specific tab. In multi-profile setups, `getAnyConnection` returns an arbitrary connection — if it differs between the add and delete calls, or differs from the connection that actually dispatched the CDP command, the capture entry is never cleaned up.
acceptanceCriteria: In `enable-network-capture.ts`, the tab's owning connection (from `getConnectionForTab` or the dispatch result) is used consistently for both adding and removing from `activeNetworkCaptures`
In `analyze-site/index.ts`, the same connection is used for both the `enableNetworkCapture` dispatch and the `activeNetworkCaptures` tracking
When the owning connection cannot be determined, the function falls back gracefully (logs a warning) rather than using an arbitrary connection
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/enable-network-capture.ts
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/index.ts

Context:
In `enable-network-capture.ts`, the pattern to look for:
```typescript
getAnyConnection(state)?.activeNetworkCaptures.add(args.tabId)
```
This should use the connection that owns `args.tabId`. `getConnectionForTab(state, args.tabId)` returns the owning connection from `state.browserTabOwnership`. If `state.browserTabOwnership` is empty (never populated), fall back to `getAnyConnection` with a warning.

In `analyze-site/index.ts`, the `enableNetworkCapture` dispatch and `activeNetworkCaptures.add` may use different connections. Capture the connection reference once (before the sleep) and use it in the finally block for `disable` and `delete`.

The `getConnectionForTab` function is in `state.ts` — check its signature and how it reads from `state.browserTabOwnership`. The browserTabOwnership map is populated by `browser_list_tabs` when called.

Changes:
1. In enable-network-capture.ts: replace `getAnyConnection(state)?.activeNetworkCaptures.add(...)` with:
   ```typescript
   const owningConn = getConnectionForTab(state, args.tabId) ?? getAnyConnection(state);
   if (owningConn) owningConn.activeNetworkCaptures.add(args.tabId);
   ```
   Use the same `owningConn` for the corresponding `delete` on disable.

2. In analyze-site/index.ts: capture the connection before the sleep:
   ```typescript
   const captureConn = getConnectionForTab(state, tabId) ?? getAnyConnection(state);
   if (captureConn) captureConn.activeNetworkCaptures.add(tabId);
   ```
   In the finally block: use `captureConn?.activeNetworkCaptures.delete(tabId)` — the same reference.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the `dispatchToExtension` call patterns — only the `activeNetworkCaptures` tracking
- `getConnectionForTab` and `getAnyConnection` are in state.ts — import them if not already imported

userStories.title: Fix NaN sort in extension-get-logs and too-restrictive hostnameFromPattern in plugin-list-tabs
userStories.description: Two independent browser-tool bugs: (1) extension-get-logs.ts sorts merged log entries with `b.timestamp - a.timestamp`, but log entries where `timestamp` is missing or non-numeric produce NaN, causing unstable sort order. (2) plugin-list-tabs.ts `hostnameFromPattern` only matches `*://<host>/*` — patterns like `https://*.example.com/*`, `http://localhost:3000/*`, or `*://example.com/path/*` are not matched, so multi-instance tabs with non-wildcard-scheme or port patterns are never annotated with instance names.
acceptanceCriteria: In extension-get-logs.ts, the sort comparator handles missing/non-numeric `timestamp` values by treating them as 0 (or sorting them to the end), never returning NaN

In plugin-list-tabs.ts, `hostnameFromPattern` correctly extracts the host from patterns including `https://<host>/*`, `http://<host>/*`, and `*://<host with port>/*`; patterns with wildcards in the host (e.g., `*.example.com`) return the non-wildcard portion or are excluded
